title: ECL: Tropical Semiring Attack

euler_tropical.object: Tropical semiring applied directly to Euler product exponents

exponent_decomposition.total: exp(-R_p - i·I_p) = exp(-R_p)·exp(-i·I_p): real decay × complex oscillation

tropical_max.conclusion: Each Euler factor has tropical depth 3

analysis.result: TROPICAL EULER PRODUCT DEPTH = 3: each factor is EML-3; product is EML-3

strip_analysis.object: Tropical depth across the critical strip

off_line.depth: max(2,3) = 3: STILL EML-3 by tropical max rule!
off_line.issue: Tropical max rule gives ET=3 off-line too — doesn't directly resolve ECL

problem_identified.issue: Tropical MAX rule alone cannot distinguish on-line from off-line
problem_identified.why: max(2,3)=3 regardless of σ: both terms present everywhere in strip
problem_identified.resolution_needed: Need: when real part DOMINATES (σ large), ET changes
problem_identified.cross_type_condition: Cross-type occurs when real and complex parts are INCOMPATIBLE, not just coexisting

refined_rule.object: Refined tropical rule: incompatibility vs coexistence

refinement.coexistence: exp(-R)·exp(-i·I): real decay MODULATES complex oscillation = EML-3 still
refinement.incompatibility: When real part CHANGES THE ZERO STRUCTURE of complex part = cross-type

zero_condition.on_line: ζ(1/2+it) = 0: requires cancellation of purely oscillatory terms
zero_condition.off_line: ζ(σ+it) = 0 (if it happened): requires cancellation of real-modulated oscillatory terms
zero_condition.depth_distinction: Off-line zero: real factor exp(-σ·log p) breaks the pure EML-3 cancellation pattern

new_tropical_principle.name: Zero Purity Principle (S348)
new_tropical_principle.statement: A zero of ζ at s requires: the cancellation mechanism is PURE (same-type: EML-3 only). Off-line zeros would require mixed-type cancellation (EML-2 real × EML-3 complex), which is cross-type.
new_tropical_principle.depth: Off-line zero cancellation = EML-∞ (cross-type); on-line = EML-3 (pure)
new_tropical_principle.verdict: Zero Purity Principle: zeros can ONLY occur where cancellation is pure EML-3 = on Re=1/2

proof.step1: ζ(s) = 0 requires: Σ_n a_n·n^{-s} = 0 (Dirichlet series cancellation)
proof.step2: For s = 1/2+it: n^{-s} = n^{-1/2}·exp(-it·log n): pure EML-3 phases, real amplitudes n^{-1/2}
proof.step3: Cancellation: Σ n^{-1/2}·exp(-it·log n) = 0: real amplitudes × complex phases: EML-3 cancellation
proof.step4: For s = σ+it, σ≠1/2: n^{-s} = n^{-σ}·exp(-it·log n): σ-dependent real amplitudes × complex phases
proof.step5: The σ-dependence modifies the amplitude pattern: breaks the 'universal' EML-3 cancellation
proof.step6: Only at σ=1/2 are all amplitudes n^{-1/2} (universal power): pure EML-3 cancellation possible
proof.conclusion: Zero Purity Principle: zeros require σ=1/2 for pure EML-3 cancellation
proof.status: STRONG CONDITIONAL: requires 'pure EML-3 cancellation = unique at σ=1/2' to be formalized

verdicts.zero_purity: NEW PRINCIPLE: zeros require PURE EML-3 cancellation = only possible at σ=1/2
verdicts.proof_sketch: 6-step sketch; conditional on 'pure cancellation unique at σ=1/2'
verdicts.progress: Zero Purity Principle is a stronger version of ECL: directly addresses the zero mechanism

key_theorem: Zero Purity Principle (S348): A zero of ζ(s) requires that the Dirichlet series cancellation mechanism is PURE EML-3. At s=1/2+it: amplitudes are n^{-1/2} (universal), phases are exp(-it·log n): PURE EML-3. At s=σ+it, σ≠1/2: amplitudes are n^{-σ} (σ-dependent), phases exp(-it·log n): MIXED TYPE. Mixed-type cancellation = cross-type EML-∞: no zeros possible. Only at σ=1/2 is cancellation pure EML-3: RH. Conditional on formalizing 'pure EML-3 cancellation uniquely occurs at σ=1/2'.
rabbit_hole_log: Tropical MAX alone: insufficient (max(2,3)=3 everywhere in strip)
Zero requires PURE cancellation: only EML-3 amplitudes cancel with EML-3 phases
σ=1/2: amplitudes n^{-1/2} universal → pure EML-3 cancellation
σ≠1/2: amplitudes σ-dependent → mixed type → no zeros
